submission.title: The worst proxy of all time, history has been made

comments.body: I get that he missed where the base was because the top mineral patch was not mined. But what made him think the base was over by where he placed the pylon? Was it just process of elimination?
As usual, scrub Brood War progamers follow the lead done by SC2 bronze geniuses : [When Cheese Fails 101 Episode 8](https://youtu.be/G-3ZrXOrz_0?t=214)
Lmao
Based on the orientations, this seems to be a 3-base map, so there was only the one other spawn location.
Yeah. Only two places the terran could have spawned.
Ah, what a classic. I was thinking about exactly this video too
Big oof
As someone who follows motives games on artosiscasts. I was devastated :(
Artosis was right, sometimes you lose because you're good.

Rain reacted in a vod and said he was supposed to check two patches because there would only be 7-8 workers out of 9 patches. Poor Motive. He deserved to be there but he let Rush have such an easy day.
This is some When Cheese Fails shit

Artosis is brutal, holy shit. 

>How embarrassing.

There's an episode where something almost identical happens. One player sees a worker leaving a base early in the game when scouting, assumes that's where the opponent spawned, and cannon rushes (IIRC) the base. Turns out the worker they saw was the other player's scout leaving an empty base.
Motives face was absolutely emotionless 😶 got to give props to that 😂
Also check out [yesterday's post by jinjin](https://www.reddit.com/r/starcraft/comments/1f4yecu/pros_react_to_motives_clown_game_vs_rush_ssl_2024/) with Korean pros' reactions to it.
This is Immediately what I thought of. The synchronized laugh gets me every time
That was so painful I felt bad for Motive on his first SSL
Man I feel terrible for this guy. All that training and practice just to have this happen. I really hope he can be gentle on himself and keep moving forward despite this.
Jesus man they’re letting that dude fucking have it 😂
Artosis is honest.
where can I catch them cast this tournament live?

This is absolutely fantastic, but I don't know if it's *the* worst proxy of all time. 

For your consideration, [Golden's proxy hatch vs Squirtle](https://youtu.be/PXz9nNOA39I?t=304) from GSL Open Season 3 in 2010. Golden accidentally builds an evolution chamber instead of a spawning pool. Then follows up with the most hilariously ineffectual attack. Even for 2010 standards, this game was notoriously bad.
I did feel that’s a bit much. They wouldn’t say that in physical sports
isnt there a game of idra proxy hatching the wrong location or something like that? I swear I remember an old pro sc2 game where something like this happened
I had that exact episode in mind, hence the reference
My man! Their videos aren't hitting the same anymore these days but they're still at it!
[Season 1 Episode 8](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G-3ZrXOrz_0), absolute classic
Oh no!
That was my favorite episode. My friend and I were crying we were laughing so hard.
Soop ap, then search ssl

Can someone explain what his thought process was? He checked the one mineral patch and confirmed it hasn't been mined, then went ahead and proxied there anyways. Think I'm missing something because it seems to suggest the player is not at that spawn.

He has dealt with that shit for so long I bet it came out of his mouth without thinking just like on streams lmao

Edit: grammar

So here's the thing - he did scout, but in a extremely meta way - he sent the worker just within vision of the top mineral patch - If you click on the patch and see minerals have been mined from it - you are in the right location without having risk expose your worker being spotted by the enemy worker - 

the unfortunate thing for him is he didn't double check the other spawn, he just assumed one wasn't worked on so the other one must be. - the mineral patch he checked while unmined was NOT worked on by the other player, for efficiency, so of course it wasnt touched thus the rush was misplaced only because he gambled hard on the reasoning the first location was empty and there was only one other spawn point for the enemy
Sad thing is Motive is a really good player but he could win an SSL and we'll remember him for this.
He can still win. Why do they keep saying it’s over

Yeah, you are missing something.  It is a 3 spawn map so as soon as he saw that the top patch had 1500, he immediately proxied the other main, not the same one.

In the pros' reactions they said you need to check 2 mineral patches just in case this happens, because at that scout timing the opponent should have 7 workers, leaving 1 mineral patch open.
Because in order for him to win, his opponent would need to destroy his own buildings on purpose, or maybe lose consciousness or something.

this is my fav ep

  
[https://youtu.be/G-3ZrXOrz\_0?si=0JdFEslcoQstpfcX](https://youtu.be/G-3ZrXOrz_0?si=0JdFEslcoQstpfcX)
Sad story
Been really enjoying their recent content, and they’ve been doing great with the short form content too
Oooooooooooof
That sounds delicious, I bet he was so salty he could single-handedly liberate India from the British all over again.
It’s another version of the Julyzerg game from way back.
I felt so bad for him...but also you live by the cheese you die by the cheese 🧀
Just to add: in the translated Korean pros video they mention you are meant to check 2 mineral patches to avoid this, so he definitely made a significant mistake here.
When keeping it Cheese goes wrong.

What I don't get is, why not send the scouting probe a bit further on the spawning position he suspected to be empty? In his mind there's no opponent there to spot him and that way he would have made sure, or in this case realised he was wrong.
This guy outsmarted himself) But nice strategy still.
Im not entirely sure if it was Idra. Now that I think about it I'm probably mixing up Idra's rage-quit-throw with the bad proxy I'm thinking about. Ima try to find it.
They have absolutely roasted people worse than this in "physical sports".

Can anyone explain what happened here?

I'm familiar with the game from way back, just never got super into the multiplayer.
man I miss watching tastosis

Was it the game where he leaves after he sees a bunch of hallucinations? I think it's colossus and he just quits. Pretty sure he had a supply lead too. The casters are just like "uhhh I think he had that game?"

Everyone fan boys over Serral but Idra will always be my favorite Zerg. Players back then had so much more personality. Everyone seems like robots to me now.
I was thinking too if you decide it's not that base and you're going to send the probe home, why not throw the probe all the way in to 100% confirm anyway
I feel so bad for him I hope to see him again he was fun to watch in the last season of CNSL
Then the opponent knows you are there and might check for proxy

But his theory here was that the enemy was *not* there. Confirming this is the case doesn't hurt since if the enemy *truly* isn't there they can't detect him. He wasn't gonna build there anyways if the hypothesis was true. He was just returning the probe.

In other words: The enemy can not detect your probe if the enemy isn't there, if that's the hypothesis. Confirming the hypothesis might well detect your probe if it was wrong, but the consequence of that (which is to be detected) is better than what happened here: complete failure.

So, just confirming the "enemy isn't at this patch" hypothesis would have been the save play.

Personally, I think he just messed up the scout. Checking 3 patches would 100% confirm anyways without ever having to see an opponent unit or structure. And in his spawn that was the only base he needed to check to determine location. He got too excited and busted early. lol

He pulled his probe in vision just to see the top mineral patch, clicked it to see if it had been mined. Didn’t want to go in further in case it was spotted 

As it still had its full 1500 he assumed that his opponent wasn’t there, so proxied his buildings outside the other spawn. 

Except his opponent WAS at the first spot

For efficiency iirc you actually want to mine that patch, it’s the second most optimal patch 

So in a way Rush not doing the optimal thing actually saved his bacon in this game
That was vs Huk on Metalopolis in WoL

To add to this: to an unskilled or lower-rank player, this looks salvageable because it’s still early in the game and, while his buildings are out of position, they can still produce units just fine. What difference does it make if they’re on the wrong side of the map?

The difference is that these are extremely high-level Korean players where a mistake this large is game-ending. The proxy player has invested a huge amount of their initial build (time, resources, and positioning) to the wrong play. Meanwhile, their opponent is making all of the right moves, knows where their opponent is, has out-produced and out-teched them, and is set up to steamroll them in the next couple of minutes. And the proxy player’s production is across the map from their base, so reinforcements will be pumping out from the wrong location. 

There is no winning here. Maybe if the opponent were a low-level scrub. But not someone of this caliber.
The saddest thing is that other than that bad luck/not double checking the spawns, the build would have put him at a big advantage.  The gateway forge was a really cool idea.

If you send out your first two probes for proxy and show one to your opponent you might as well leave.  Maybe he could have slid down the left and checked a second mineral patch but just walking in to check is a guaranteed way to throw the game
Tasteless mentioned he thinks its one of the more efficent patches, ig the scout hinged on opponents taking advantage of that.
But again the hypothesis was that there was no one there. Better check if that's the case rather than walking away with game changing false information (which is what happened here).

Say you will check the base.  Two scenarios: they are there or they are not there.  If they are there, you just lost the game by showing your proxy.  If they are not there, the result is the same as not checking.  Going down to check is never a beneficial move.  Going down the left to double check on a second mineral patch is the only way to do it without throwing.

If you assume the enemy is not there, it doesn't hurt to check, does it? Because if you are right, there is noone there to spot you.

Here the guy didn't do exactly that and lost.

I don’t think you’re understanding that if you go check and your assumption is wrong you’re literally throwing the game.  You may avoid proxying in the wrong base but the game is still over if you send your first two probes out to proxy and it gets spotted immediately.  If your assumption was correct then it literally changes nothing.  There is no reason to send your probe down to double check.

So the scenario we are talking about is you send 2 scouts, top right you cannot check minerals, so bottom you check minerals. You see it isn't mined (which is what happened in the game). From this position motive assumed it was top right and sent the bottom probe home.

What we are suggesting is that instead of sending it home, send it further into the base to 100% confirm. As you point out, yes this is a disadvantage, however, you then decide to not put the gate in the top right base. You are still massively behind, but less behind than having a gateway in the complete wrong base and putting a forge and cannon in that base.
This exactly.

On the contrary.

What you don't seem to understand is that we are suggesting a zero risk solution for preventing the catastrophic loss situation we can see in this video, that is, *preventing* to completely throw the game by building in the wrong "assumed" base because the assumption where the enemy is *not* was not confirmed.

Two possibilities:
1. Assumption was correct and the enemy is not there: nothing happens. Noone spots you. 
2. Assumption was incorrect and the enemy is in fact there: Enemy might spot you and you have a disadvantage. However that disadvantage is significantly lower than what happened in this video since you will not proceed to build in the wrong base.

What is the problem with this tactic? Let me know.

They absolutely do. I still remember this: https://youtu.be/9bBns6ZUn0U?si=UudlvN0YDF1uek-Z

“That’s the most ridiculous thing I’ve ever seen. Patrick Stefan, you should be embarrassed for what you just did! That does not belong in the NHL.”

This isn’t some obscure broadcast either it’s one of the main Canadian ones.
I stand corrected

Can someone give me, a first time viewer of anything StarCraft, a better understanding?

I get he thought the mineral wasn’t being mined, but I don’t know what decision he made because of that and why it mattered

(While you said you do get the part about mining, I'll still include that to fully present Motive's thought process.) 

Yellow player Motive playing the Protoss faction sends out two of his starter mining and construction units, a probe, to scout out where the enemy base is. Motive knows there are only three possible spawn locations on the map to start in. Motive is obviously at one of those spawn locations, so that means their opponent, orange player Rush, can only have their base in one of two places. 

Motive sends two probes because he is planning to construct buildings aggressively and they want to get that started as early as possible. Their probe checking the southwest corner arrives at its destination first. Motive doesn't want Rush to know he is being scouted, so they stay just outside of the range of mutual visibility for any of Rush's units. Theoretically, Motive can know where Rush is mining without their mining units seeing each other by being just in range to see the minerals being mined. Every mineral field starts the game with 1,500 that can be mined and anybody can click on a mineral field to see how many minerals are left. 

The trouble is that Motive only checked the top-most mineral field, which is the furthest away from the HQ building that minerals have to be brought back to, and therefore tends to be left alone anyway until there are more mining units to fully saturate the mining operation. If Motive checked any other mineral field, then they would have seen they were being mined. Instead, Motive worked off of that sample size of one, the least optimal one, and assumed the base couldn't be at that location.

Since Motive erroneously ruled out the possibility of the base being in the southwest corner, there is only one other possible spawn point that Rush could have started in: along the eastern side of the map. Motive already has a probe nearing the eastern spawn location ready to go. However, instead of checking any of the mineral fields over there to verify that they are in the right place, Motive immediately starts his attack plan. 

Motive builds right next to the spawn location just outside of the range of mutual visibility. First commanding the probe to build a pylon, which gives Protoss structures power to operate and allows structures to be built in the first place. Then builds a gateway, which allows Motive to spawn infantry right next to the enemy base instead of walking them across the map. Then builds a forge, which expands the technology tree to allow for the building of photon cannons, which are incredibly strong defensive structures. (You can also cheese enemies by building photon cannons just outside an enemy base, then build them closer and closer until your "defensive structures" are inside the enemy base.) So then Motive starts building the photon cannon.

This is when Motive realizes the mistake, because the photon cannon being built is close enough to see the vespene gas geyser, which is the other gatherable resource aside from minerals and is required for Rush to build more powerful units like tanks. But to gather vespene gas, Rush would have to build a gas refinery on it. Which should have been done by this point. But it is clearly still just the geyser there. So Motive finally has the probe scout out this other spawn location and finds that it is empty. This proxy base being constructed is in fact no closer to the enemy than the main base is and is also in the middle of nowhere. Useless for attacking the enemy while at the same time also being useless for defense. Just a huge waste of time and money.

Most players would quit two and a half minutes into the video knowing that the rest of the game would be played at a crippling disadvantage.
Thanks a ton for the detailed explanation! I know I’ll never get into StarCraft, but it’s such a big game and seems so interesting so it’s nice to get more context

The point of contention here is this.  
You say:

>Enemy might spot you and you have a disadvantage. However that disadvantage is significantly lower than what happened in this video since you will not proceed to build in the wrong base.

And I am saying:

>You may avoid proxying in the wrong base but the game is still over if you send your first two probes out to proxy and it gets spotted immediately.

i.e. You say revealing the probe puts you at a disadvantage, albeit a playable one.  I say revealing the probe puts you at an unwinnable disadvantage.

I am not exaggerating when I say the game is over.  There is roughly a 0% chance you win the game after your proxy gets spotted if you send two of them out that early.  It doesn't matter if you proxy in the wrong base or show your probe, both scenarios are both instant losses.

We both clearly understand the four different scenarios given scouting/not scouting and opponent being there/not being there, so there's no need to bring that up in the discussion anymore.  The core clash between our arguments is that I am saying it is not a "zero risk" play to show the probe.  You can argue back that showing the probe would not lead to an auto-loss.  That would be addressing the difference in assumptions that lead to our different conclusions.

This is one of those moments where he was too precise for his own good. Click on a mineral patch so you can save one second and completely avoid detection? Great... until you accidentally misjudge their base location and proxy in the completely wrong spot - was tough but fun to watch haha
You still can!

What is Mengsk

"**Arcturus Mengsk** was a [terran](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Terran) from [Korhal](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Korhal). He led the [Sons of Korhal](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Sons_of_Korhal) and orchestrated the downfall of the [Terran Confederacy](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Terran_Confederacy) during the [Great War](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Great_War). Mengsk ruled the [Terran Dominion](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Terran_Dominion), the Confederacy's successor, as [Emperor](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Emperor_of_the_Terran_Dominion) Arcturus Mengsk I,[^(\[4\])](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Arcturus_Mengsk#cite_note-SC:BW_E5_M1-4) until his death during the [Second Great War](https://starcraft.fandom.com/wiki/Second_Great_War)."

submission.title: TY vs Rain grudge match confirmed? (v=h2MxuCcA7Qk)

comments.body: Wow I didn't know SSL was this spicy, I might just have to watch it now.

It's just rebranded ASL.  Basically same format as GSL.  This is ro16 group select and all the players are long time friends.  TY is actually quite good at BW and made it into ASL last season which is incredibly impressive given the skill level of the top BW players.  He brought some SC2 concepts to his group stage matches and knocked out Jaedong.  If given a week to prepare a build I would give him a pretty good chance to beat Rain.

I knew about TY being good in both SC but it's really fun to see these kinds of challenges and verbal jabs between players. 


These days in SC 2 only Reynor or Byun has some spicy remarks regarding others and I loved watching MC especially cause of this. It almost feels robotic with the rest of the players.

Most recommended way to watch is on Youtube on the AfreecaTV Esports channel for Tastosis or StarcastTV for Nyoken + rotating guest caster from the BW community, usually a high level foreign player.  Videos are uploaded ~2 days after the broadcast for Tastosis and ~half a day later for StarcastTV.  You can find the live stream link on teamliquid.net on the Events/Features sidebar when it’s broadcasting.  To find when the game is played, check the same sidebar under Upcoming Events or the tournament’s Liquipedia page.

Nah not always, it's common in ZvT to see mutas but it makes more sense to do on some maps than others, and some players might opt for builds/ strategies that just don't utilize mutas much at all.
Can anyone explain why there are random sunken colonies in areas with no creep??
It's a trick of the mapmakers. The base creep and the neutral sunken connect, effectively spreading the creep to the base entrance so zergs have a fair chance at defending their base.
